codeReview.summary: Commit d988ae3 resolves the two prior blocking issues from skill-ops-sync: verifySync now detects a portable SHA-256 command on both the local machine and remote host, and syncSkill now includes HostConfig.keyPath in the rsync SSH transport. The added tests cover the new helper logic and verify that the remote checksum command uses portable detection; I did not find a regression in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill's procedure/feedback loop is still inaccurate for this run: the worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` true even though the transcript shows implementation edits before the first test-file edits, and the handoff omits the manual SSH verification that the skill requires for SSH-related features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/shuv/repos/shuvdex/.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-30,80-86` requires tests before implementation and manual SSH verification for SSH-related features. In worker session `e3a04f72-7ec5-4c3b-8957-8a736ee14bff`, the transcript skeleton shows `Edit` operations on `packages/skill-ops/src/live.ts` before the first `Edit` on `packages/skill-ops/test/skill-ops.test.ts`, and no `ssh shuvtest`/`ssh shuvbot` command appears. The handoff at `/home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-15T20-04-08-460Z__fix-skill-ops-portability__e3a04f72-7ec5-4c3b-8957-8a736ee14bff.json` records only npm-based verification commands while still setting `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` to true.

summary: Reviewed fix feature `fix-skill-ops-portability` as a rerun for the prior `skill-ops-sync` failure. The diff in commit `d988ae3` adequately fixes the original portability/keyPath blockers, and I found no new blocking issues in the reviewed change set.
